author: codex

patch.value: Ratchet command-layer coverage above the 59 percent baseline

The July 16 modernization run established 888 passing tests and 59.26% aggregate line coverage. Core API and formatter modules are near-complete, but many Commander action callbacks are exercised only by optional live-server E2E tests and remain low-coverage in isolated CI. Add dependency-injected command harnesses in coherent slices, establish a non-regressing coverage floor, and raise it incrementally without requiring live Jellyfin credentials.

Record and enforce a non-regressing baseline for functions and lines.; Add isolated command tests by domain until aggregate line coverage exceeds 80 percent.; Keep changed command/formatter modules at or near 100 percent and retain read-only live E2E coverage.
Prevent regressions in user-facing command orchestration while preserving fast isolated CI.
Makes global option propagation, validation, error formatting, and request wiring measurable across every command family.
A ratcheted repository-wide coverage policy with command modules covered by isolated tests and live E2E retained as supplemental proof.

value.author: codex
value.text: Baseline from bun test --coverage on 2026-07-16: 888 pass,177 credential-dependent E2E skip,44.69% functions,59.26% lines; changed session formatter 100% functions and lines.

message: Capture measured command coverage improvement roadmap without claiming it active

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Bring existing coverage lineage into current Jellyfin API delivery epic

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Claim mandatory repository-wide 100 percent coverage work

author: codex-jellyfin

patch.value: A genuine 100/100/100/100 coverage contract enforced repository-wide.
Eliminates the current untested command-callback surface and prevents future uncovered production code.
Make every shipped behavior and failure path executable, reviewable, and regression-proof.

Statements, branches, functions, and lines each measure exactly 100 percent across all source code; Coverage thresholds are enforced globally and per-file in every release and pull-request gate; Tests assert observable behavior and failure paths without coverage-only no-op execution or duplicated cases; Read-only live E2E, packaged-binary smoke, acceptance, performance, and dogfood suites are mandatory documented gates; Coverage cannot be reduced through ignore directives, source exclusions, or threshold changes without explicit governance failure

Replace the legacy 59 percent command-layer ratchet with a mandatory, non-bypassable 100 percent threshold for statements, branches, functions, and lines across every source file. Coverage must come from meaningful unit, integration, functional, acceptance, smoke, dogfood, performance, white-box, black-box, and read-only live E2E tests; exclusions and artificial execution-only tests are not acceptable.
Measured baseline is 44.93 percent functions and 58.83 percent lines; the user has elevated full coverage to the highest-priority release requirement.

message: Raise existing coverage lineage to mandatory 100 percent four-dimension gate

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Baseline measured with bun test --coverage on 2026-07-21: 898 pass, 177 skipped, 0 fail; 44.93% functions and 58.83% lines. Bun's text reporter exposes only functions/lines, so the mandatory gate must use a four-dimension instrumenter and per-file thresholds while preserving Bun compatibility.

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Link current schema-resilience implementation as first strict-coverage slice

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Highest-priority requirement accepted. Current Bun coverage reports only functions and lines; evaluating LCOV/four-dimension enforcement and coverage capture for command subprocesses before selecting the non-cheatable gate.

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Connect coverage ratchet to historical and live execution proof

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Coverage remains the principal release blocker. Bun coverage reports 58.83% lines and 44.93% functions; Vitest can express all four 100% thresholds but its Node runtime currently fails 254 tests across 14 Bun-specific suites using Bun.spawn or Bun.serve. No artificial exclusions or mocked coverage inflation will be accepted.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Decision: retain this feature in progress until genuine 100/100/100/100 source coverage is demonstrated. The next implementation step is a runtime-neutral test process/server harness plus exact branch inventory, not threshold weakening.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Latest post-change coverage run: 901 pass, 177 live-only skips, 0 fail; 58.64% lines and 44.56% functions. Command callbacks execute primarily in subprocess acceptance tests and are not attributed to the parent Bun coverage profile, which is the dominant instrumentation gap.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Bun 1.3.11 exposes text and LCOV reporters but only native line/function threshold dimensions; Vitest exposes four dimensions but does not merge Bun child-process coverage automatically. Reaching the governed target requires in-process command execution or an explicit child-profile merge, followed by real missing-branch tests.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Continuing implementation on 2026-07-22 after full all-status inventory; will measure current exact coverage first, close meaningful gaps, and verify read-only installed-binary E2E against the private configured instance.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Quality rule: coverage work must assert observable behavior and failure paths; no ignore directives, artificial execution-only cases, or duplicated tests.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Portability foundation completed: typed Node adapters make 894 deterministic tests pass under Vitest; authoritative four-metric baseline is 38.19 statements, 37.40 branches, 49.92 functions, 37.87 lines. Live compiled CLI read-only suite passed 177/177. Item remains in progress because genuine 100/100/100/100 is not met.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Bun subprocess acceptance tests provide strong black-box behavior proof but child execution is not attributed to parent coverage. Keep them mandatory and separate; use typed Node-compatible harnesses plus in-process command tests for deterministic four-metric attribution.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: 2026-07-23 diagnostic after official TOON work: portable Vitest gate passes 906/906 with 38.24% statements, 37.38% branches, 50.00% functions, and 37.90% lines. The 100/100/100/100 acceptance requirement remains open and is not claimed complete.
